entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content:
It's been two months since I introduced the abapgit-agent. Here's a summary of what landed since then (v1.10 → v1.19.1):

New Commands

run — Execute an ABAP program or a class implementing IF_OO_ADT_CLASSRUN directly from the terminal — no SAP GUI needed

abapgit-agent run --class ZCL_MY_RUNNER

transport — List, create, check, and release SAP transport requests

abapgit-agent transport list
abapgit-agent transport release DEVK900001

lint — Run abaplint static analysis on changed files, offline, no ABAP connection needed

abapgit-agent lint --base main

drop — Physically delete a single ABAP object from the system (useful before a clean re-pull)

abapgit-agent drop --files src/zcl_broken.clas.abap --pull

customize — Write a row to a SAP customizing table (delivery class C/E)

abapgit-agent customize ZAPP_CONFIG --set KEY=FEATURE_X VALUE=active

Smarter Pull

  • Conflict detection — Pull now detects branch switches and object conflicts before activating, configurable via .abapgit-agent.json
  • Async pull for large changesets — Pulls with >10 files automatically execute via a background job to avoid HTTP timeouts
  • Transport selector — Choose which transport request to use, with hook support for CI pipelines
  • --sync-xml — After pull, syncs XML metadata back to local files

Better Testing & CI

  • unit now uses the ADT API — Richer output: test class name, failure details, execution time per method
  • Coverage threshold enforcement — Define minimum coverage % in .abapgit-agent.json; CI gate fails if not met
  • --junit-output on both inspect and unit — Emit JUnit XML for Jenkins/GitHub Actions integration
  • inspect improvements — Variant from project config, exclude/suppress rules, chunked requests for large file sets, and --doc to fetch SCI check documentation inline

Deeper Debugging

  • Debug --expand TABLE[N] — Expand nested table variables and field symbols during a debug session
  • Scripted debug scenarios — AI agents can now drive debug sessions end-to-end: set breakpoint → attach → step → inspect → continue
  • In-process debug daemon — More reliable attach/detach; no more frozen SM50 work processes after REPL disconnect

More Object Types

view and pull now support: DOMA, MSAG, FUGR, DCLS, SUSO (authorization objects), ENHO (enhancement implementations), and XML-only objects (TABL, STRU, DTEL, TTYP)

Quality-of-Life

  • abgagt shortcut alias (saves keystrokes)
  • Auto-recovery from expired SAP sessions (no more manual re-auth mid-workflow)
  • --version / -v flag
  • --verbose flag for HTTP error body details
  • view --full --lines for dual line numbers (global + include-relative) used by AI to set precise breakpoints
  • Bundled guidelines + guide --migrate — The ABAP development guide is now bundled inside the CLI instead of copied into your repo as numbered files (guidelines/01_..., guidelines/02_..., etc.). If you're on an older project, run:
    abapgit-agent guide --migrate
    This removes the old guideline files, replaces CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md with slim stubs, and keeps any project-specific files you had under guidelines/. The full guide is always available on demand via abapgit-agent guide.

Install / Upgrade

npm install -g abapgit-agent@latest
abapgit-agent init --update

init --update refreshes your CLAUDE.md with guidance for all new commands.

Full docs: https://sylvoscai.github.io/abapgit-agent/

entry.title: Commodity Management in the Chemical Industry: Why It Matters and How SAP Addresses It
entry.content:
Chemical companies - whether they produce fertilizers, petrochemicals, polymers, or industrial acids - operate in one of the most volatile and financially complex sectors of the global economy. Unlike typical manufacturing businesses, where raw material prices are relatively stable and procurement is straightforward, the chemical industry depends on commodities whose prices can change dramatically from one day to the next.

Managing this complexity is not just a logistical challenge - it is a strategic imperative. That is where Commodity Management comes in, and why SAP has built a dedicated suite of capabilities directly into its ERP platform to address it.

What Is Commodity Management?

Commodity Management is a specialized set of modules and functions designed to handle the unique complexities of procuring, pricing, trading, and managing commodity raw materials and finished goods - goods whose prices are subject to market volatility.

A commodity is a good that is actively resold on organised global markets, including stock exchanges. Commodities are characterised by:

  • Large trading volumes (tonnes, cubic meters)
  • Global standardisation and interchangeability
  • Prices driven by the balance of global supply and demand
  • A high proportion of forward and futures transactions
  • Significant stock exchange turnover

For the chemical industry specifically, commodities include acids, ammonia, monomers, basic polymers, and fertilizers - all of which are inputs or outputs of complex, high-volume production processes that span multiple continents and supply chain tiers.

Key insight: While standard procurement handles buying and selling finished or semi-finished goods at relatively stable, negotiated prices, commodity management deals with the reality of fluctuating market prices, complex logistics, and significant financial risk - all at the same time.

Why Is Commodity Management a Distinct Business Discipline?

Five core characteristics make commodity management fundamentally different from standard supply chain and procurement operations:

  • 1 Price Volatility
    The price of a raw material like naphtha, ammonia, or potassium chloride can shift by the minute. Contracts cannot simply carry a fixed price — they must be linked to a market index, such as "LME Copper + differential." Companies that fail to account for this exposure put their margins at serious risk
  • 2 Complex Pricing Mechanisms 
    Commodity pricing is rarely a single number. It typically involves a provisional price set at the time of shipment and a final price determined later — once quality analysis is complete, delivery is confirmed, and market conditions for a specific averaging period are known. The formula can be multilayered, incorporating index averages, differentials, quality bonuses or penalties, and surcharges.
  • 3 Financial Risk Exposure 
    Price fluctuations create major financial exposure. A company that purchases a commodity today for future delivery and faces a market price drop has overpaid — potentially significantly. Managing this exposure through financial instruments such as futures, options, forwards, and swaps is a core discipline in commodity-driven businesses.
  • 4 Regulatory Compliance and Complex Accounting 
    Trading commodities — especially when financial derivatives are involved — requires strict regulatory oversight and adherence to complex accounting standards such as Mark-to-Market (MtM) revaluation and IFRS hedge accounting rules.
  • 5 Multi-Modal Bulk Logistics 
    Commodities are moved in bulk: by vessel, pipeline, rail, and truck. Differences between the loaded quantity and quality and what is received at the destination directly affect the final invoice. Capturing these actualisations accurately is essential for correct financial settlement.

A few numbers

  • 65% of companies do not have, or are unsure they have, the correct technology to face current market volatility
  • 30% cite control and transparency as a primary driver for technology change
  • 30% cite competitive advantage as a key driver for upgrading their CTRM platform

Source: Commodities People Energy Trading Annual Survey 2023

The Chemical Industry Value Chain: Where Commodity Management Operates

Chemical production chains are long and complex. Consider a fertilizer producer. The process starts with the mining and enrichment of mineral raw materials such as phosphorus and potassium. These are then transported to chemical plants, combined with gases (nitrogen, sulphur), processed through acids production, and ultimately formulated into complex fertilizer products (NPK, NPKS). Each of these steps involves commodity purchases, multi-modal logistics, and price-sensitive contracts.

Similarly, in petrochemicals, naphtha sourced from oil refineries feeds cracking plants that produce monomers like ethylene and propylene - which in turn become polymers, plastics, and downstream consumer products. At every step, there are commodity contracts, market-priced inputs, shipping logistics, and risk exposure that must be actively managed.

Commodity Management applies across two primary zones in these value chains:

  • Procurement side: buying raw materials at market-linked prices from external suppliers and managing the price risk between contract signing and final delivery
  • Sales side: selling finished goods at commodity-indexed prices to global customers and distributors, managing delivery terms, quality adjustments, and final invoicing

SAP's Solution Portfolio for Commodity Management

SAP offers what it describes as the world's only commodity management solution built directly into an ERP platform. This embedded approach eliminates the need for costly, complex integrations between separate CTRM (Commodity Trading and Risk Management) tools and the ERP system - replacing a fragmented landscape of spreadsheets, risk tools, logistics systems, and ERP with a single platform.

The core principle: One platform. One source of truth. End-to-end control from trade to cash.

The SAP solution portfolio for commodity management spans several integrated components:

CM for Physical Contracts

Manages commodity procurement and sales contracts with market-linked pricing, provisional and final invoicing, quality-driven price adjustments, and period-end revaluation. Fully embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP Private.

Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE)

Enables complex, formula-driven pricing rules linked to market data. Supports fixed, indexed, combination, differential, quality-adjusted, and partial-quantity pricing - all within a single flexible framework.

CM Risk Management

Provides exposure management, hedge management, Mark-to-Market analysis, Value at Risk (VaR), and full hedge accounting support compliant with IFRS standards. Uses capabilities of SAP Treasury and Risk Management.

Global Trade Management (GTM)

Manages end-to-end trading document flows including trading contracts, sales and purchase orders, document links via the Trading Execution Workbench, and position management by trading contracts.

SAP for Oil & Gas / TSW (Option for Oil and Gas Companies)

The Trading Scheduler Workbench (TSW) handles bulk logistics: nomination and ticketing, inventory planning, supply chain visualisation, berth planning, 3-way pegging, and integration with commodity management for risk exposure capture.

SAP Transportation Management (Option for Chemical, Mill Products, Mining and Agriculture Companies)

Provides Logistics Planning and Execution capabilities for Chemical Producers including Bulk Transportation. Compatible  

Global Trade Services (GTS)

Manages compliance, import/export controls, sanctioned and denied party screening, Intrastat, and broker communications - all natively integrated with SAP S/4HANA ERP, EWM and TM.

These components operate within the broader SAP Cloud ERP Private architecture, which integrates commodity management with Source-to-Pay (MM), Order-to-Cash (SD), warehousing, transportation, finance and accounting, cash management, and treasury - all coordinated through the Business Technology Platform

 

 

What Makes SAP's Approach Unique?

The fundamental differentiator is native integration. Commodity data - prices, risk positions, contract terms, logistics actualisations - flows in real time through a single data model. There is no reconciliation of separate systems, no duplicate master data, no complex interfaces to maintain between a standalone CTRM tool and the ERP. This delivers three key outcomes:

  • Profitability: Full transparency and accurate invoicing through the commodity pricing engine, increased visibility into risk and hedge performance, and improved accounting and costing compliance.
  • Integration: A single set of master data, transactions, and prices across all functions - procurement, trading, logistics, risk, and finance - with workflow and role-based security throughout.
  • Transparency: Real-time commodity supply chain planning, better and faster decision-making through commodity analytics, and a consistent, uninterrupted flow from logistics to risk to finance.
AI Feature

AI-Powered Capabilities: The Intelligent Commodity Work Center

SAP has introduced AI-assisted capabilities directly within commodity management workflows, powered by SAP Joule, SAP's generative AI copilot. These capabilities are designed to accelerate deal creation, reduce manual effort, and help traders work more efficiently.

The AI-Assisted Commodity Work Center (ACW) supports the following:

  • Draft document generation: Joule can create draft deal documents for transaction types including Purchase, Sale, and Buy & Sell - as well as Agricultural Contract Management (ACM) trading contracts for 3rd Party Purchase and 3rd Party Sales.
  • Multi-item and formula-based pricing support: Traders can instruct Joule to generate drafts with one or multiple items, using fixed prices or index/formula-based pricing.
  • Search by field similarity: The AI identifies similar documents already in the system based on key deal fields, allowing traders to leverage previous contracts as the basis for new ones - reducing setup time significantly.
  • SAP Fiori interface: The work center is built as a modern Fiori app, delivering a personalised, responsive, and intuitive user experience optimised for efficient trading operations.

Looking further ahead, Joule agents are positioned to orchestrate end-to-end commodity business processes - from deal capture through to logistics execution and financial settlement - within the SAP Cloud ERP Private architecture.

Customer Adoption in the Chemical Industry

SAP's commodity management solutions are already deployed across leading chemical companies globally. Customers such  NOVA Chemicals and others are live on SAP Commodity Management and reporting on business benefits. These real-world deployments validate the maturity and scalability of SAP's approach for the chemical sector.

Why Now?

The economic and geopolitical landscape of the 2020s has made commodity risk management a board-level priority. Supply chain disruptions, energy price shocks, currency volatility, and tightening environmental regulations have all amplified the importance of having accurate, real-time visibility into commodity exposure. The survey data cited above reflects an industry that knows it has a technology gap - and is actively seeking to close it.

SAP's commodity management portfolio - embedded in S/4HANA, co-innovated with leading chemical producers, and increasingly enhanced with AI - is positioned to close that gap for the chemical industry.

Key Takeaways

Commodity Management is a mission-critical business discipline for chemical producers and traders. It addresses price volatility, complex formula-driven pricing, financial risk exposure, compliance requirements, and multi-modal bulk logistics — all in an integrated way.

SAP offers the only commodity management solution natively embedded in an enterprise ERP, combining physical contract management, a commodity pricing engine, risk management, bulk logistics (TSW or Transportation Management), and global trade compliance into a single platform on SAP S/4HANA.

AI-powered capabilities through SAP Joule are now accelerating trader workflows, with AI-assisted deal creation and similarity-based document search available in the Commodity Work Center. And more of AI capabilities to come - please stay tuned!

More Information:

SAP Commodity Management, SAP for Chemicals

 
Sergey Nozhenko, SAP Industry Business Unit – Chemicals

Hi,

I chose RabbitMQ as my broker because it is lightweight, reliable, and highly portable and I could run locally via Docker. Since I am building this on a personal Linux environment, I wanted a stack that allows for full offline development while remaining compliant with the Cloud Events specification.

Using the MQTT plugin for RabbitMQ allows bridging traditional messaging with a Python-based pipeline.

entry.title: Re: Navigate to a Specified URL After Exiting a Performan...
entry.content:
Hi @adeeb_rizvi1,

I've tested this extensively, and you've hit on the exact pain point: manual vs. dynamic links.

From a Systems Engineering perspective, you should never manually construct these URLs. The 'Clean Core' way to handle this is through Tokens. If you add &returnURL=www.yourlink.com to the end of the [[DOC_ACCESS_URL]] token in your E-mail Notification Templates, SuccessFactors will do the heavy lifting of injecting the unique fid for every single user.

One tip from my research: Always ensure your returnURL is URL-encoded (e.g., use %3A%2F%2F instead of ://). If the system sees unencoded characters, the redirect often fails and defaults back to the standard Performance Inbox.

Quick question: Are you planning to send these links via email, or are you hosting them on an internal company dashboard?

entry.title: Re: SI3: Logistics_WM 兼容性包问题
entry.content:
您好 @evae ，

我的朋友，如果您的 SAP 就绪性检查显示 SI3：物流_WM (LE-WM) 受影响，则表示您的系统仍在使用 S/4HANA 兼容包中的仓库管理 (WM) 功能，而这些兼容包正在逐步淘汰。要解决此问题，您必须将 LE-WM 替换为 SAP 支持的替代方案，并在兼容性到期日之前停止使用 WM 事务。

要解决此问题，请按以下步骤操作：

确定仍在使用的 WM 功能。使用 SAP 就绪性检查 → 兼容性范围分析和 SAP EarlyWatch Alert → 兼容性使用到期报告（列出过去 3 个月使用的 WM 事务）。这将告诉您哪些 WM 流程必须替换。

选项 1 — 迁移到库存室管理 (SRM)

SRM 是 LE-WM 的“技术继任者”，用于基本仓储管理。它包含在 S/4HANA 中，无需额外许可证。

如果您仅使用 WM 进行上架、拣货、调拨单等基本仓库流程，则此方案适用。

方案二 — 升级到扩展仓库管理 (EWM)

如果您使用 WM 的高级功能，例如波次管理、堆场管理、货位分配、劳动力管理以及复杂的入库/出库流程，则必须升级到 EWM。

EWM 的部署方式：嵌入式 EWM、分散式 EWM

希望这能帮助您解决问题。如果我的解决方案对您有帮助，请采纳。如果您有任何疑问，欢迎随时提问。

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to show only leaves in SAC Excel add-in table?
entry.content: Hi, I’ve run into something similar before. Unfortunately the SAC Excel Add-in doesn’t currently offer the same “Show Leaves Only” behavior that’s available in SAC stories. In Excel, the hierarchy expansion is handled differently, so parent nodes like “ALL” and “2025” often still appear even if you only want the month members. One workaround is to flatten or filter the hierarchy level directly in the model/query if possible, or use member filtering to restrict the dimension to the lowest level only. Some people also disable hierarchy display entirely in the table settings when they only need month-level values. I recently read an article about [AI call agents and business process automation](https://flowster.app/ai-call-agents-the-next-frontier-in-business-process-automation/) and it reminded me how many enterprise tools still handle hierarchy and automation logic differently across interfaces — web apps vs add-ins can behave surprisingly inconsistently. Hope you find a clean workaround!

entry.title: Re: Extensibility options for SAP Real Estate in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Dear @Magda_RR,

In Public Cloud only the Contract and Lease Management (CLM) part of classic RE-FX is in scope. Classic RE master data Business Entity, Property, Building, Rental Unit, Pooled Space is not part of Public Edition SAP's strategic solution for that side is Planon Real Estate and Facilities Management for SAP which became an SAP Solution Extension in January 2026. The limited number of Business Contexts and released Badis you see is a consequence of that scoping

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-real-estate-and-facilities-management/ba-p/14310012

On your first point recommended extensibility approaches for FI-impact RE scenarios. The supported model is the standard three tiers Key User Extensibility (Custom Fields, Custom Logic against released Business Contexts), Developer Extensibility (Embedded Steampunk, ABAP Cloud, 3-system landscape) and Side-by-Side on SAP BTP. User-exits and classic modifications are not supported (clean core SAP Note 3578329). In practice for FI impact you work in this order configuration first (expense types, account determination, valuation parameters) then cloud Badis contract-side RECN_CONTRACT_CHECK_ALL / RECN_CONTRACT_SUBSTITUTE / RECE_VALUATION_PARAMETER, FI-side the standard journal entry Badis in the Custom Logic app under Finance / Posting and finally the OData posting API for anything outside a Badi hook

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3578329

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_FOR_REAL_ESTATE/1ccb507ddc6b4191bc1695c2d2995af5/cc59e124fed04627aee1eeaf2e91d53b.html

On your second point released APIs, Badis and events for RE beyond what is in the system. The contract-side Badis you have already found are essentially the delivered toolkit. There is no separate set of RE-specific accounting Badis FI-impact logic goes through the standard FI extension surface. The relevant delivery on the FI side is the Accounting Business Transaction Interface introduced in 2502, with configuration items Define Real Estate Expense Type (106882) and Define Account Determination (106906) and the OData V4 API Real Estate Cost Allocation Accounting Notification Post Journal Entry for posting from external or side-by-side logic, traceable in Manage Accounting Notification (F7289). Release 2508 extended cost settlement across architectural structures, usable/rentable objects and CLM contracts. No RE-specific business events are published on Business Accelerator Hub today

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-contract-and-lease-mgmt-product-amp-strategy-updates-latest-innovation/ba-p/14370862

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/finance-with-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2502/ba-p/13957394

https://api.sap.com/package/S4HANACloudBADI/overview

https://api.sap.com/products/SAPS4HANACloud/overview

On your third point roadmap and best-practice documentation for customer-specific accounting logic. The canonical reference is Implementing Contract and Lease Management in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud System on SAP Help Portal, together with the relevant scope items (1T6, 21P, 34N, 21Q, 2SA, 2SB, 34O, 2O9, 7EZ, 7G4, 7G5, 21R) and the 2502 / 2508 FI and CLM release notes. On the roadmap side the direction publicly delivered so far is the Accounting Business Transaction Interface and the cost settlement extension anything beyond that is best tracked in SAP Roadmap Explorer

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/70747b45a7ab457b8eafbf4474a20d0c/b7138d1ebe19469a889137bc1abd3ae5.html

On your fourth point the missing Business Context for RE objects as Usable Objects. This follows from the scoping decision above. The classic RE master data layer is not in scope of Public Edition, so a Business Context exposing it for reuse is not delivered. What is exposed Rentable Object, CLM Contract, contract objects is available via released CDS views and OData services visible in ADT and on Business Accelerator Hub. There is no public commitment to deliver an RE master data Business Context if the requirement is concrete and recurring the right channel is SAP Customer Influence campaign 2759

More details below:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2759

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Technical Inquiry on Account Differentiation via BADI...
entry.content:
Dear @S0024835231,

BADI_ACE_DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_CLD is a supported Public Cloud BAdI for Purchase Order Accruals, but its scope is the final Accrual Engine journal entry line item, not the original PO item. SAP confirms that this BAdI can be used to modify journal entry line fields such as G/L account, cost center and profit center for PO Accruals postings

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3068511

For this reason, I would not position BADI_ACE_DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_CLD as the enhancement point to directly read PO item attributes such as Product Type Group / Policy Type. If the account logic must depend on such PO-level information, this value must first be available in the Accrual Engine context. SAP shows the same design pattern for PO-derived information such as Trading Partner the value is brought into the Accrual Engine using custom field / field mapping logic and then used during Accrual Engine posting

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3256557

For PO Accruals logic that depends on PO item classification or whether an accrual object/item should be created the more relevant enhancement points are the PO Accruals-specific BAdIs for example POAC_ACCRUAL_OBJECT_ITEMS and POAC_PO_ITEM_CLASSIFICATION. SAP confirms POAC_ACCRUAL_OBJECT_ITEMS for influencing PO accrual object items in Public Cloud

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3544598

Conclusion:

BADI_ACE_DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_CLD can be used to dynamically override the final debit/credit G/L account on Accrual Engine posting lines. It should not be used as the main PO item classification BAdI. For Policy Type / Product Type Group-based account logic the recommended design is to derive or map the policy value into the Accrual Engine context first, and then use BADI_ACE_DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_CLD only for the final G/L account adjustment

Best Regards,

Dawid
